Notice Of Motion And Motion For Award Of Attorneys' Fees, Costs, And Expenses
Off Calendar Jul-25-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JOHN BERNARD's Motion For Award Of Attorneys' Fees, Costs, And Expenses.

Plaintiff's "motion for award of attorneys' fees, costs, and expenses" in this lemon law case is granted in the total amount of $80,000. To avoid further time consumption, this includes fees for reply papers and any oral argument.

The opposition largely focuses on "excessive" discovery motion practice. But this was due to defendant's own discovery recalcitrance, which ended only after the court gave defendant "one more chance" rather than grant terminating sanctions. (Memo. 7:25.) Notably, plaintiff did not seek fee enhancement, though he might well have.

The fee award is reduced to account for quibbles over lead counsel's hourly rate and the level of work he performed. (See Serrano v. Priest (1977) 20 Cal.3d 25, 49 ["experienced trial judge is the best judge of professional services rendered in his court"].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

Motion granted. The responses were served after the deadline and all objections have been waived. Defendant shall provide further responses without objections by January 26, 2024. Defendant retains the ability to file a separate motion for relief from waiver.
